preview.summary: World Cup debutants Uzbekistan take on a loaded Colombia side in a Group K opener at the Azteca. Expect a disciplined, defense-first Uzbek block against Colombia's pace and creativity on the wings, with the South Americans heavy favorites to start their campaign with three points.
preview.homeForm: Making their first-ever World Cup, Uzbekistan (FIFA #50) lost only one of 16 matches in AFC qualifying and play with tactical discipline.
preview.awayForm: Colombia (FIFA #13) scored 28 goals in CONMEBOL qualifying, second only to Argentina, and built a long unbeaten run into the tournament.
preview.homeKey: Eldor Shomurodov — captain led qualifying with five goals and four assists.
preview.awayKey: Luis Diaz — Colombia's pace on the wing and seven qualifying goals.
storylines: Uzbekistan's historic first World Cup match
Colombia's wing speed vs a packed Uzbek defensive block
James Rodriguez pulling the strings as Colombia's assist leader
preview.h2h: The sides have rarely met, and this is their first encounter on the World Cup stage.
